philosophy: Recognition over beauty. Interruption over reflection. Truth over aspiration.
single_test: Would someone stop scrolling because they recognize this as their own life — not because it is beautiful?
schema_note: Scenes are organized by creative category, not hook type. Generation priority selects scenes by audience temperature and awareness level. No character registry for object_shots or environment_shots.

object_shots.label: Object Shots
object_shots.description: One specific object in its natural context. No person visible. The object tells the entire story through recognition alone.

generation_rules.style_anchor: iPhone snapshot of something you noticed while passing by — not arranged, not styled. Shot by someone who grabbed their phone to capture something true. Slightly imperfect angle. One corner of the object may be cut off.

O1.name: gym_bag_car
O1.scene: A gym bag sitting in the back seat of a car — packed and ready but clearly not going anywhere today. A coffee cup in the cupholder, keys on the seat. Daylight through car windows. Bag has been there a few days. Slightly dusty.
O1.composition: Shot from the front seat looking back through the gap between headrest and seat. Bag centred but not perfectly. Upholstery and car interior providing context — slightly cluttered, receipts, a jacket.
O1.lighting: Natural daylight through car windows. Slight window glare acceptable. Not studio. No flash.
O1.do_not_show: Gym branding on bag clearly visible, gym signage through window, any person in the car, active workout context, aspirational car interior

O2.name: running_shoes_unused
O2.scene: A pair of running shoes — still white, barely worn — sitting by the front door next to regular everyday shoes. Laces still tied from last time. A coat or bag nearby from normal life.
O2.composition: Shoes at floor level, shot from slightly above at a casual angle. Everyday context around them — doormat, umbrella, other worn shoes. Horizontal iPhone framing.
O2.lighting: Hallway or entryway indoor light. Could be morning or evening. Warm and real.
O2.do_not_show: Brand logos clearly visible on shoes, any fitness tracking device, person wearing or holding the shoes, aspirational shoe styling

O3.name: fitness_app_streak
O3.scene: A phone screen showing a fitness or habit tracking app with a broken streak — the current streak is 0 or 1 days, but a longer streak is visible from months ago. The phone is resting on a surface, not being held actively.
O3.composition: Phone screen fills most of frame. App streak chart visible but no identifiable app branding. Resting on couch arm or desk. Slight angle — not perfectly flat.
O3.lighting: Screen glow as primary light. Ambient room light. Evening or early morning context.
O3.do_not_show: Identifiable app logos or brand, person holding the phone, fitness transformation imagery on screen, readable personal data

O4.name: gym_bag_door
O4.scene: Gym bag propped against a front door or hallway wall — unzipped slightly, showing what is inside but clearly not leaving the house today. Water bottle tucked in the side pocket. A pair of shoes beside it.
O4.composition: Bag fills most of the left side of frame. Door or wall behind it. Slightly messy context — real hallway, not staged. Close enough to see the bag is packed but not going anywhere.
O4.lighting: Indoor hallway light — morning. Warm overhead.
O4.do_not_show: Any person visible, gym branding dominant, bag being actively packed or unpacked, aspirational context, staged styling

O5.name: calendar_monday
O5.scene: A physical paper calendar or planner — Monday is circled or starred in a week that has a hand-written note about working out or going to the gym. The note was clearly written weeks ago. The surrounding days are filled with real life — appointments, grocery lists, normal things.
O5.composition: Calendar flat on desk or pinned to wall. Close enough to see the note but not clearly readable. Coffee ring stain acceptable on corner. Slightly warped paper — real use.
O5.lighting: Desk lamp or window light. Morning feel. Warm and lived-in.
O5.do_not_show: Gym branding on calendar, any specific gym name visible, digital calendar, aspirational desk styling

O6.name: water_bottle_cabinet
O6.scene: A full, unopened water bottle sitting in a kitchen cabinet — behind coffee mugs and normal kitchen items. Still full. Has been there for a while. Slightly dusty cap.
O6.composition: Cabinet open, water bottle visible among everyday kitchen items. Not centred perfectly — caught in a moment of noticing. Close crop. Cabinet context clear.
O6.lighting: Kitchen interior light — cabinet slightly darker than main kitchen. Warm ambient.
O6.do_not_show: Brand logos on bottle clearly visible, gym context in surrounding area, any person visible, aspirational kitchen styling

environment_shots.label: Environment Shots
environment_shots.description: A specific space that tells a story. No person visible, or person seen only from behind. The space itself is the subject. Recognition comes from the environment, not a face.

generation_rules.style_anchor: Documentary-style. Shot by someone walking into a space and capturing it before it changes. Not arranged. Slightly imperfect angle. Hand-held feel — horizon not perfectly level.

E1.name: car_parked_gym
E1.scene: Inside view of a car parked in a gym parking lot — steering wheel and dashboard in foreground, gym entrance visible through windshield at mid-distance. Engine off. No one in the car. A coffee cup in the cupholder. Morning light.
E1.composition: Shot from the passenger seat or rear seat looking forward through the gap. Gym visible through windshield — close enough to be recognisable but not close enough to read signage. Dashboard provides grounding. Real car interior — not clean, not staged.
E1.lighting: Early morning or late afternoon. Low sun through windshield. Dashboard instruments off. Windshield slightly fogged at corners.
E1.do_not_show: Any person in car, gym signage that clearly displays a real brand, anyone entering the gym, any motivational imagery, clean aspirational car interior

E2.name: kitchen_6am
E2.scene: A kitchen at 6am — dishes from last night still in the sink, coffee maker running, one light on. A gym bag visible in the corner of the room but the counter has the chaos of a morning already getting away from someone.
E2.composition: Wide kitchen shot. Counter with coffee maker as anchor. Sink with dishes in background. Bag visible at edge of frame but not the focal point. Slightly cluttered — real home, not staged.
E2.lighting: One overhead kitchen light on. Early morning exterior light barely starting through window. Warm but dim and slightly yellow.
E2.do_not_show: Any person visible, magazine-ready kitchen, organised aspirational morning context, gym equipment as focal point, aspirational lifestyle staging

E3.name: bedroom_corner
E3.scene: A bedroom corner with home exercise equipment — dumbbells, a resistance band, a yoga mat still rolled up — that clearly has not been used in weeks. A pile of clothes draped over a nearby chair.
E3.composition: Corner of bedroom. Equipment on the floor or a small rack. Chair with clothes beside it. Real bedroom — not a home gym shoot. Equipment is background context, the corner tells the story.
E3.lighting: Bedroom natural light from window. Morning. Slightly warm.
E3.do_not_show: Any person visible, organised home gym aesthetic, equipment that looks new and aspirational, brand logos clearly visible

E4.name: parking_lot_morning
E4.scene: A gym parking lot at early morning — a few cars parked, the gym entrance visible at mid-distance but nobody going in right now. The frame captures the stillness of a moment between decisions. No person visible.
E4.composition: Wide shot of parking lot. Gym entrance at mid-distance. A few cars scattered. Empty spaces. Morning light through. No close-up of signage — gym is context, not subject.
E4.lighting: Early morning — first light. Cool tones. Long shadows across the tarmac. Quiet.
E4.do_not_show: Dominant gym branding clearly legible, any people visible, active workout context, anyone entering or leaving the gym, aspirational gym exterior shoot

interrupted_action_shots.label: Interrupted Action Shots
interrupted_action_shots.description: A person caught mid-something — never sitting quietly, never posed, never looking into the camera with intention. The subject is always in the middle of an action that got interrupted by a thought or a moment. Real people, real bodies, real expressions.

generation_rules.style_anchor: Candid — like someone took the photo mid-conversation. The subject does not know this is a photo worth keeping. Slightly motion-blurred at edges. Not perfectly in focus.

P1.name: mid_eyeroll
P1.scene: Person caught mid-eyeroll or mid-exhale — not at the camera, reacting to something they just thought of or heard. Kitchen or living room. The expression is wry and real, not performed. This is the face of someone who has made this joke to themselves before.
P1.subject: Adult, 38-52, real body type, casual everyday clothes. Mid-expression — the eyeroll is the moment, not the setup for one.
P1.emotion: dry self-awareness — the face of someone who knows exactly what they keep doing and finds it slightly funny
P1.lighting: Kitchen or living room natural light. Daytime window light. Not overhead fluorescent. Warm and real.
P1.composition: Three-quarter portrait, slightly off-centre. Expression is the subject. Person is mid-motion, not settled. Slightly imperfect framing as if the camera caught this accidentally.
P1.do_not_show: Camera-facing pose, full planned smile, athletic context, gym setting, anyone who appears to be performing an expression for a camera

P2.name: phone_stop
P2.scene: Person has stopped mid-scroll on their phone — they just hit something that made them pause. Not reading — just paused. Their expression shifts slightly. Could be standing in a kitchen, sitting on a couch, or standing in a hallway.
P2.subject: Adult, 38-52, phone in hand, body partially turned or stationary — not posed, just stopped. Real clothes, real body.
P2.emotion: the half-second of recognition before you decide whether to keep scrolling or stop
P2.lighting: Interior home light mixed with phone screen glow. Evening or early morning.
P2.composition: Three-quarter shot. Phone visible in hand. Face partially visible — focus is on the pause, not the face. Subject is in the middle of the frame but not centred intentionally.
P2.do_not_show: Screen content visible or readable, gym app context on screen, posed phone-scrolling, anyone who looks like a stock photo model, camera-facing gaze

P3.name: walking_in_from_behind
P3.scene: Person walking into a gym from behind — we see only the back of their head and shoulders, gym entrance ahead of them. The walk is normal, unhurried — the ease of someone who belongs here now. Not dramatic. Just someone going in.
P3.subject: Adult, 38-52, seen from behind only. Casual gym-appropriate clothes but not athletic gear — just someone going somewhere they know. Real body type visible from behind.
P3.emotion: quiet belonging — this is just what they do now
P3.lighting: Gym entrance light — morning. Natural light from outside mixing with gym interior light.
P3.composition: Shot from behind and slightly to the side. Person off-centre. Entrance ahead — visible but not the focal point. Candid angle, not perfectly framed.
P3.do_not_show: Person's face visible, posed entry shot, athletic gear, dominant gym branding, anyone looking at camera, stock gym shoot aesthetic

P4.name: mid_laugh_real
P4.scene: Person caught mid-laugh during a real conversation — not performed, not at the camera. They are laughing with someone off-camera. Could be at a gym, a coffee shop, or a kitchen. The location is secondary — the laugh is the content.
P4.subject: Adult, 38-52, real body type, casual clothes. Mid-laugh, looking off-camera at whoever they are talking to. Completely unaware of the camera.
P4.emotion: genuine amusement — the kind that happens when you are comfortable somewhere and something actually lands
P4.lighting: Natural light. Could be indoor or outdoor. Warm and real. Not studio.
P4.composition: Three-quarter portrait. Subject is mid-motion, head slightly turned, completely unaware of being photographed. Slightly imperfect crop — as if the photographer was also mid-laugh.
P4.do_not_show: Camera-facing laugh, posed smile, gym equipment prominent, anyone who looks like a fitness model, staged group interaction

P5.name: coach_member_real
P5.scene: Coach and member caught in a real moment mid-conversation — the coach is explaining something with hands, the member is listening and nodding. Not a training session. Could be in a gym hallway, near the front desk, or at a coffee shop. The relationship is the subject.
P5.subject: Coach figure, 30-45, and member figure, 38-52, both in real everyday clothes or casual gym attire. Not a training pose. Both look like actual people, not stock fitness models.
P5.emotion: genuine connection — someone who is actually seen and heard by another person
P5.lighting: Natural light — gym entrance, window, or coffee shop. Not studio lighting.
P5.composition: Both people in frame. Neither is posing. The coach's gesture and the member's attention are the story. Medium shot. Equal visual weight.
P5.do_not_show: Workout in progress, gym equipment dominant, athletic body showcase, posed coaching moment, anyone who looks like a stock photo personal trainer, camera-facing poses

text_creative.label: Text Creative
text_creative.description: Portal-rendered text only — not generated by Flux. These templates are rendered by the marketing-portal as styled social posts. No image generation required.

T1.name: single_line
T1.description: One bold statement. White text on dark background or brand colour. High contrast. No imagery. Used for cold and pattern-interrupt placements.

T2.name: three_line_offer
T2.description: Offer breakdown: Line 1 = the problem, Line 2 = the solution, Line 3 = the offer. Used for retargeting and most-aware audiences.

retired_permanently: solo portrait reflective — person gazing into distance with staged emotion
person gazing out window — beautiful light, melancholic expression
person alone with coffee in beautiful light — curated morning stillness
cinematic golden hour portrait — backlit subject in field or outdoors
woman sitting alone feeling something — soft focus, private moment
gym interior as primary setting for cold audience (awareness levels 2-3)
before/after visual framing of any kind
person looking at bathroom mirror dramatically
parent watching child play from a distance — pain-point distance metaphor
